characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Four-Temperament Ensemble: Carly is the most outgoing and friendly (Sanguine), Sam is the most temperamental and selfish (Choleric), Freddie is the most introspective and realistic (Melancholic), Spencer tends to change his mood on a dime (Eclectic), and Gibby is the most polite of the bunch (Phlegmatic). Freudian Trio:Ego: Carly is the caring oneSuperego: Freddie is the technical producerId: Sam in is it for herself Ego: Carly is the caring one Superego: Freddie is the technical producer Id: Sam in is it for herself

characters.name: As a Whole
characters.description: Four-Temperament Ensemble: Carly is the most outgoing and friendly (Sanguine), Sam is the most temperamental and selfish (Choleric), Freddie is the most introspective and realistic (Melancholic), Spencer tends to change his mood on a dime (Eclectic), and Gibby is the most polite of the bunch (Phlegmatic). Freudian Trio:Ego: Carly is the caring oneSuperego: Freddie is the technical producerId: Sam in is it for herself Ego: Carly is the caring one Superego: Freddie is the technical producer Id: Sam in is it for herself

characters.name: Carly Shay
characters.description: The central protagonist andStraight Manto the constant shenanigans surrounding her, Carly is a relatively normal highschooler. At least, as normal as youcanbe when you're an Internet celebrity. Abandoned Catchphrase: In the first season, when upset, she used to say "Aw, Come on!" A-Cup Angst: Early on in the original series, she seemed rather insecure about her flat chest, particularly when Sam made fun of her helping bra. She eventually matured as the show went on. However, when she rediscovers George the bra in "iStart Over", she laments how she never grew into it. All Girls Want Bad Boys: She falls for charming delinquent Griffin in "iDate a Bad Boy". However, she loses her attraction to him when she discovers that he collects "Pee-Wee Babies." All Love Is Unrequited: To Freddie in the original series, at least until the end of Season 2. He has always had feelings for her, but she initially didn't love him back and kept kindly turning him down, merely seeing him as a friend. Subverted in Season 3 when she starts to become enamored with him herself.

characters.name: Samantha "Sam" Puckett
characters.description: Carly's best friend and petite blonde co-host of theiCarlywebshow. In this trio, Sam very definitely takes on the "Brawn" side: she's aggressive, violent, andeats everything. She has a very tenuous relationship with her mom, who wasThe Ghostuntil finally introduced in Season 4, played by none other thanJane Lynch. Action Girl: She's quite athletic and willing to resort to violence to get her way. She's known in her school for being a good fighter, even able to defeat boys. Ambiguously Jewish: Mentions Jews and Judaism frequently, has a fetishistic obsession with ham and bacon, uses Jewish inflections such as "oy", and mentions having a Bat Mitzvah in one episode.Subverted, according to this exchange from "iBust a Thief."Sam:That laptop is the only nice thing I've got. It was a Bat Mitzvah gift from my grandmother.Freddie:But you're not even Jewish.Sam:(grabs Freddie's shirt): You tell my grandma that and I'll break you.Freddie:(scared): Shalom! Amicable Exes: She and Freddie remain good friends after a failed attempt at dating. Badass Adorable: A petite blonde who is also very strong and can kickanyone's butt. She is very threatening, despite her cute appearance.

characters.name: Fredward "Freddie" Benson
characters.description: The cameraman and general tech-support guy for iCarly, as well as Carly's other best friend and childhood crush. He is an all-around nice guy, along with growing up under the wing of hisoverbearing motherMarissa Benson. All of the Other Reindeer: He's picked on, teased, and bullied by both his classmates and peers at school andespeciallySam, though she does tone it down in later seasons. In the original series, his friends seem to enjoy mocking him and get really annoyed with him quite easily, even if he hasn't done anything wrong. Amicable Exes:With both girls, but especially with his childhood crush Carly, whom he maintains a good friendship with into their adulthood despite not having seen the latter in years.He's also on good terms with his second ex-wife Gwen, wishing her nothing but happiness after revealing that she's engaged to someone else. With both girls, but especially with his childhood crush Carly, whom he maintains a good friendship with into their adulthood despite not having seen the latter in years. He's also on good terms with his second ex-wife Gwen, wishing her nothing but happiness after revealing that she's engaged to someone else.

characters.name: Spencer Shay
characters.description: Carly's quirky but loving older brother and legal guardian, as their parents are offdoing Godknowswhat(Mr. Shay is explicitly in the armed forces, but that's all that's been said). He is a sculptor of some sort, but easily out-wacky-hijinxes the younger characters despite his more-advanced age. Achievements in Ignorance: In between the end of the original series and the revival, Spencer becomes wealthy after his marshmallow sculpture of the White House melted which Freddie interpreted as a brave statement against America's crumbling democracy. However, it turns out it was an accident as Spencer's cell phonesomehow caught on firenear the sculpture. Acquainted with Emergency Services: He is not just acquainted with the fire department, the fire departmentblocked his number. Age-Gap Romance: In the revival, he briefly dates Harper's cousin Maeve. Spencer's around forty at this point, while Maeve is presumably in her late-twenties. Age-Stereotypical Food: The childish Spencer likes to make fun food for himself and his sister Carly, such as spaghetti tacos, showing his creative and child-like mind. In "iChristmas", when Carly wishes he was "normal", he becomes a serious and mature adult lawyer, who makes boring food like steamed halibut.

characters.name: Orenthal Cornelius "Gibby" Gibson
characters.description: Appearing as a minor character when the series began, Gibby is among the main trio's friend and schoolmate and a frequentButt-Monkeyguest in the webshow. As the show progressed,he was slowly developedto be one of the gang's most loyal friends and a usual sidekick to Carly's eccentric big brother Spencer. Alliterative Name: Averted, because Gibby is just a nickname. Ascended Extra: Originally was just meant to have a one-time appearance. He then started to apear more often while having an occasionalmoment in the limelight.He then got outright promoted to being part of the main cast starting with season 4. Beware the Silly Ones: Despite being a goofball, Gibby knows boxing and is capable of throwing a mean punch. When he finds out that his friends are captured by Nora, Gibby mounts a rescue and take on the psycho fangirl. Big Damn Heroes: Pulls this off iniPsychoto save the gang from theAx-Crazyfangirl Nora.

characters.name: Harper Bettencourt
characters.description: Audience Surrogate: Her lack of knowledge about theiCarlywebshow puts her into the shoe of newcomers to the series. Competition Freak: She and Spencer compete with each other quite often. In "iRobot Wedding" they have a competition to see who can score the most dates at Nevel's wedding. "iLove Gwen" takes it even higher. The episode opens with them playing pool, with Harper outright admitting that she doesn't like pool and only plays in order to beat Spencer at it. Millicent exploits their need to outdo each other so that they'll help her with her school's production ofRomeo and Juliet(Spencer designing the sets and Harper making the costumes), promising them that there is a prize for whoever does the best job. Carly also exploits it in order to trick Spencer and Harper into doing puzzles with Mrs. Benson so that she and Millicent wouldn't have to. All she had to do was say that Spencer is the best at puzzles, with Harper unable to resist the challenge. Contrasting Replacement Character: Harper takes Sam's role as Carly's new best friend, but she could not be more different from her predecessor: Sam was a white girl who never put effort into anything other than iCarly and grew up to live her life as a free sprit, from running a babysitting service to joining a biker gang. Harper is a Riches to Rags black girl working a dead-end job at Skybucks while working to achieve her dream as a fashion designer and has no involvement in iCarly since a big part of her character is about making her own success and working hard rather than riding Carly’s coattails. If she ever is, she's only there for the ride. Dead-End Job: After her family went bankrupt, Harper has been working as a barista at Skybucks Coffee Bar with minimum wage for four years. She quits said job in "iMLM", as it was interfering with important fashion events. Determinator: Harper may have lost everything when her family went bankrupt. But, she still moves on to fulfill her dreams.

characters.name: Millicent Mitchell
characters.description: Alliterative Name:MillicentMitchell. Ambiguously Brown: She has dark brown skin, but her ethnic background hasn't yet been established. Her actress is half-black and she mentions studying Capoeira, perhaps implying Afro-Brazilian heritage. Her biological mother, Gwen, is also shown to be of an unknownAmbiguously Brown(likely Latin American) ethnicitynoteGwen's actress, Carmela Zumbado, is Cuban., though she's still much lighter skinned than Millicent, so her deceased birth father was most likely black. Bratty Teenage Daughter: Not yet a teenager, but still tends to be rude to everyone, especially Carly and Freddie. Brilliant, but Lazy: Millicent is quite clever and crafty, but she often tries to take shortcuts to her success, like trying to cancel Carly. Deadpan Snarker: She's probably the snarkiest of the cast in the revival, arguably moreso than Freddie. Carly tends to have that rivalry with her.

characters.name: Marissa Benson
characters.description: Freddie's overbearing and overprotective mother who frequently clashes with the main characters. Action Mom: IniStill Psycho, she and T-Bo take on the Dershlit family to rescue the iCarly gang. Amazingly Embarrassing Parents: While Freddie loves his mom, her overbearing nature exasperates him to no end. Things reach their boiling point when her antics end up with him moving out in "iMove Out". She still does it 8 years later even when Freddie is a grown adult. Arbitrarily Large Bank Account: Marissa isn't particularly stated to be rich nor does she work lucrative jobs, yet she still is able to support herself and Freddie in a lavish apartment, as well as own various expensive equipment. This is subverted when T-Bo moves in with the Bensons, as he has a full-time job. Big Damn Heroes: In "iStill Psycho" along with T-Bo.

characters.name: Nevel Amadeus Papperman
characters.description: AChild Prodigywho hosts a popular review website called Nevelocity and has a strong crush on Carly. After Carly rejected his creepy advances, Nevel makes it his mission to ruin her life as payback. Abhorrent Admirer: He has an obsessive crush on Carly and makes a series of advances on her during their first encounter, none of which she welcomes. Carly was quite disturbed to learn that he was her boyfriend in the Christmas alternate universe. Affably Evil: Lampshaded by Shelby Marx, who describes him as "polite, but super creepy". Arch-Enemy: To Carly and her friends. In the original series, he’s determined to either ruin their web-show or get it cancelled. Big Bad: The closest thing the show has to a primary antagonist, and almost every episode he appears in has him stirring up a conflict, just because he has a petty grudge against Carly for rejecting him and he wants to make her miserable. He may be the most recurring threat, but in terms of scale, he doesn'tquitematch Nora Dershlit's. In fact, aSam and Catepisode has him being theLesser of Two Evilshelping Sam take down Nora a la Hannibal Lecter.

characters.name: Lewbert Sline
characters.description: The bitter and obnoxious doorman who's often pranked by the main characters. Abusive Parents: Implied. When Spencer filled in for Lewbert as doorman, he separated Lewbert's mail into three categories: from his mother, death threats, anddeath threats from his mother. Asshole Victim: Since he is such areviled person to be around, especially after he shows no sympathy for Freddie after he fell down the stairs (though it was fake), andultimately aggressively tells Spencer off, he gets beaten up by two truckers, not that anyone minded. Berserk Button: He has many, but his biggest one is whenever something spills on his floor and it gets dirty so then he has to clean it up. When Freddie fell down the stairs, Lewbert yelled at him not to bleed one drop of blood on his recently cleaned floor. Best Served Cold: He waited 10 years, cleaned himself up and attended law school for several years.All for the sole purpose of suing Carly for the torment her and her friends inflicted on him so many years ago.

characters.name: Wendy
characters.description: A student at Ridgeway who is well acquainted with the iCarly trio. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: She vanishes in season 3, after her appearance in "iFight Shelby Marx", the last episode of Season 2 (though was sparingly mentioned). Girl Posse: Two other, unnamed girls are often seen hanging around with her, although they provide a more likable example of the trope than most. Graceful Ladies Like Purple: She seems better adjusted than the main trio and tends to wear shirts or scarves with some shade of purple somewhere on them. Innocently Insensitive: During Sam's birthday roast, she (and the others) accidentally make Sam feel self-conscious about being such a tomboy when she amusedly recalls a sleepover where Sam stole her bra, filled it with pudding and put it in the freezer to harden into bolas she threatened the household with the next morning.

characters.name: Guppy Gibson
characters.description: Gibby's younger brother. Adorably Precocious Child: He has a goofy innocence and naivety about him often. Alliterative Name:GuppyGibson. Big Brother Worship: He seemed to adore his older brother initially, but it seems like now they're getting on each other's nerves. Big Damn Heroes: With his brother in "iPsycho". While Gibby takes on Nora, Guppy frees the gang by unlocking the door.

characters.name: Principal Theodore "Ted" Franklin
characters.description: The principal of Ridgeway High School. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: He's very lenient towards his students, and does not believe in discipline (to an extent). Franklin is willing to overlook Sam's poor grades and bullying of other students in favor of her contributing to the comedy oniCarly, which he is a fan of. In "iGotDetention", after Sam seriously injures Mr. Howard, Franklin accepts Sam's notion that it was an accident and not a malicious attack, which is why he was hesitant to give Sam a detention. Cloudcuckoolander: Though surprisingly, he's one of the mostsaneteachers. Crazy-Prepared: After Freddie tries getting detention on purpose by dropping Franklin's phone to the ground while he was using it, Franklin gloats that his phone is indestructible due to being made with a harder shell design and does nothing to punish Freddie whatsoever. Nice Guy: He's very (if not overly) lenient towards his students, including Sam, and evenapologizedto Sam for giving her detention as punishment for hacking into his computer. He remains friendly with Carly and Freddie when they're all grown up, even attending Carly's 27th birthday party.

characters.name: Ms. Francine Briggs
characters.description: The strict English teacher at Ridgeway Secondary School who clearly hates children. Brain Bleach: Carly and Freddie have the unfortunate timing of watching her exercise in her swimsuit and hiding behind a Randy Jackson cardboard cutout while she is rubbing her butt against it. Child Hater: It's very clear that she hates every student in the school. Disproportionate Retribution: Threatens to expel Freddie simply because Spencer, a former student she despised, is at school. Everything's Louder with Bagpipes: Quite terrible at playing the bagpipes. When she played on an episode of iCarly, the show starts to lose viewers until Freddie turns on the green screen behind her to play funny videos.

characters.name: Mr. Howard
characters.description: A malicious, mean teacher at Ridgeway High School who antagonizes the iCarly trio (especially Freddie). Asshole Victim: In "iGot Detention" Sam pounded him in the face with a football and he was bruised so badly he had to wear a retainer. Principal Franklin doesn't really feel sorry for him. Awful Wedded Life: Mr. Howard is married but it is clear that he hates his wife and the feeling is mutual. Child Hater: He directly did say "I HATE CHILDREN!" Disproportionate Retribution: He gives Gibby triple detention for being "too Gibby" and his smart-ass replies.

characters.name: Chuck Chambers
characters.description: A bratty, violent young boy who holds a grudge against Spencer for getting him grounded. Spencer considers Chuck to be his arch nemesis. Alliterative Name:ChuckChambers. Arch-Enemy: To Spencer. Bratty Half-Pint: He's a violent, disrespectful brat. Deliberately Cute Child: Acts this way to Carly in "iTwins" while she's tutoring him, before she finds out the truth about him from Spencer.

characters.name: Amanda "Mandy" Valdez
characters.description: ALoony Fanwho's obsessed with iCarly. She likes wearing duck masks and quacking like a duck. Catchphrase: "Quack quack!" Cloudcuckoolander: Stalks theiCarlys with an unhealthy obsession, even having broken into their apartment not through the door but through the windows, and they live on the fourth floor. She also has a weird habit of putting on a duck mask and quacking like a duck. Doting Grandparent: She has an affluent grandmother who Mandy bosses around into doing whatever she says to be attached to theiCarlys. Loony Fan: She thinks the appropriate way for being their biggest fan is to hang out at their house, interfere with their webcast, and constantly send them presents. She's even attached to Spencer very much, even though he's usually absent from the show.

characters.name: Terrance Jeter "T-Bo" Bo
characters.description: The Jamaican cook and cashier at the Groovy Smoothie who always pressures people to buy foods (especially bagels) in addition to smoothies. Ascended Extra: Gets promoted to secondary character in Season 6, appearing in most of said season's episodes. Berserk Button: Whenever people refuse his requests to buy food in addition to smoothies (especially bagels). Big Damn Heroes: In "iStill Psycho", T-Bo and Mrs. Benson ride all over to the Dershlit to rescue the iCarly gang after Nora kidnaps them again. Crouching Moron, Hidden Badass: Helps Mrs. Benson pull off aBig Damn Heroesto save the iCarly gang from the insane Dershlit family, beating up Mr. Dershlit singlehandedly.

characters.name: Nora Dershlit
characters.description: A social outcast with no friends other than her pet chicken Maurice. Nora is a big fan of iCarly and invites the Carly, Sam and Freddie to attend her 16th birthday party, hoping their presence will attract others to her party and it works. Turns out, Nora is a psychopath who wants to keep them with her forever, so she locks them in her basement, where they remain until Gibby rescues them. Nora is sent to prison, but is granted parole a year later and kidnaps them a second time, along with Gibby and Spencer, this time with help from her equally insane parents. Ambiguously Bi: Has a crush on Freddie, but also kisses a popular girl on the cheek at her birthday party, though it's played like she's justreallyexcited to have popular people at her party. Ax-Crazy: Lampshaded. She iscompletely insane, wields an actual ax at one point and is perfectly willing to kill. She'sworsein "iStill Psycho" and has her equally insane parents backing her up. Although she has mellowed out considerably in "iHate Carly", she still isn't entirely sane. Basement Dweller: Even as an adult, she still lives with her parents, which Freddiehypocriticallychides her for before he remembered his position. Batman Gambit: In "iStill Psycho" especially. Her plan depended on so many things- the iCarlys forgiving her and agreeing to go to her house, the judge paroling her, Spencer picking them up and needing to use the bathroom, etc.

characters.name: Socko
characters.description: Spencer's best friend. Best Friend: He's a close confederate to Spencer since at least high school, who seems to confide in him off-screen, with Socko often managing to help him out with problems in the show and the two doing a fist bump in the one episode Socko (or his arm anyway) appears. The Clan: Socko has lots of cousins, siblings, aunts uncles and the like who also do favors for Spencer. The Ghost: He's constantly mentioned but only has one confirmed appearance on the show (and that was just his hand sticking through the doorway).In-UniverseFreddie's blog lampshades his absence and claims that he's camera shy Meaningful Name: He owns a sock company, although its unclear ifSocko is his real name or not.Everyone else in the family seems to have a name that corresponds to their profession (a car salesman named Otto, a thief named Rob, etc.)

characters.name: Granddad Shay
characters.description: The paternal grandfather of Carly and Spencer from Yakima, Washington. Disappointed in You: While he loves Spencer as a grandson, Granddad Shay doesn't have a high opinion on him due to his immaturity and his descision to drop out of law school and become an artist. Even after Spencer became rich, Granddad still doesn't think very highly of Spencer. Doting Grandparent: He is a loving and caring grandfather. He dotes on his granddaughter during his visits. On his grandson, not so much (although he does still love him). Do Wrong, Right: In "iWanna Stay With Spencer", he scolds Spencer not for rubbing his stomach in public, but doing it “wrong”. Everyone Calls Him "Barkeep": Even Harper and Millicent call him Granddad.

characters.name: Duke Lubberman
characters.description: A bully wrestler at Ridgeway High School who first appears fighting another wrestler (with Freddie in between) in "iLike Jake", and gets paired with Freddie for a science project in "iHatch Chicks". Animal Lover: In "iHatch Chicks", he forcefully insists to Freddie on lifting the Shays' fridge if it means saving one of the chicks, shouting at him "I LOVE ANIMALS!" The Big Guy: Naturally for a student wrestler. In "iHatch Chicks", he rescues two chicks, one by lifting an entire fridge, and another bysmashing his head straight through a wall. Big Eater: Between being easily distracted by promises of meat, helping himself to a snack out of the Shays' toppled fridge, and abruptly running off to the Groovy Smoothie, Duke has quite the appetite. The Bully: To Freddie, briefly. He shoves his musty kneepad in Freddie's face for no other reason than "because it reeks."

characters.name: Beau
characters.description: Carly's ex-boyfriend who breaks up with her on a livestream in "iStart Over". Though, it later turns out in "iReturn to Webicon" that he still has feelings for her. Betty and Veronica: The fun-loving and spontaneous "Veronica" to Wes' cautious and practical "Betty" and Carly's "Archie". However, Carly finds Beau easier to get along with than Wes, because of how similar in personality she and Beau are. Characterization Marches On: Beau never got much characterization in the first episode. In the season finale, he's established as a dim-witted and reckless free spirit who loves to have fun above all else. Took a Level in Jerkass: Due to being rejected off-screen by Carly in between Seasons 1 and 2, he and Wes aremuchmore petty and rude towards her in "iGuessEveryone Just Hates Me Now", to the point of the two making a webshow dedicated to badmouthing Carly, and mocking her for being unable to find love when she tries to make amends with them.

characters.name: Double Dutch
characters.description: Harper's first styling client after quitting her job at Skybucks. Double Dutch is a world-famous celebrity who was once described by a magazine as "the most innovative star to be born in the year 1994". She is full of herself and a bit spacey, but she has faith in Harper's ideas.Her relationship with Harper gradually becomes less professional and more romantic as time goes on, and the two share a kiss in "iReturn to Webicon". Alliterative Name:DoubleDutch. Though it's her stage name. Asian Airhead: She's of Asian descent and is quite spacey. Informed Ability: She's an internet-famous pop star, but we've yet to see her perform. She does sing a little bit in "iCan Fix It Myself", but she sounds pretty bad (Either she wasn't giving it her all or she relies onAuto-Tune). Jerk with a Heart of Gold: She's an arrogant diva, but she does appreciate Harper and values her opinions. She also let Spencer pet-sit her dog to help him get over his breakup with Maeve.

characters.name: Maeve
characters.description: Age-Gap Romance: While her age isn't stated, she's likely around the same age as Harper, who's in her late-twenties, while Spencer is around forty. Faked Kidnapping:Maeve pretended to be kidnapped in order to avoid paparazzi and debt collectors after her family went broke. Long-Lost Relative: She is Harper's missing cousin, who was kidnapped by pirates overseas for four years.She actually faked her kidnapping after her family went backrupt and used the kidnapping as a cover story to go on a cruise ship. Love Interests: To Spencer. Unlike Spencer's previous single episode romances, she has a steady relationship with him, joining the main cast and living with him upon her debut episode. However, things quickly grow sour between them as a result of them having moved too fast and they agree to part ways. Strong Family Resemblance: Despite only being cousins, her and Harper look quite alike.

characters.name: Paul Denham
characters.description: Large Ham: He has an eccentric personality, remmiscent of a car salesman. Nice Guy: As eccentric as he is, he does wants to help Carly and his team. Though he is willing to play dirty on occasion if it means he'll make more money. Sheep in Sheep's Clothing: Zig-zagged. Despite Freddie's initial suspicions, Paul's help is genuine, if not a bit goofy, and he's truly a nice caring guy. However, it's revealed that he hid envy towards Freddie for working with Carly longer, and his moments of repeatedly showing Freddie up weren't entirely innocent.

characters.name: Pearl Wallace
characters.description: Beware the Nice Ones: She reaches her breaking point in "iLoveYour Shoes" when it's abundantly clear that Freddie and Carly still have feelings for each other, deliberately throwing an axe near Carly's head, and calling out both of them for disregarding her feelings before effectively dumping Freddie. Cloudcuckoolander: She occasionally displays rather odd behavior, such as eating bananas while they're still unpeeled or believing that a murder mystery party means a party where the theme is crows. Nice Girl: Doesn't hold any grudge against Carly when unintentionally the whole internet hates her for "breaking" Carly and Freddie's relationship (a relationship Carly made up) and is overall very pleasant towards Freddie and Millicent. Satellite Love Interest: Exists solely for being Freddie's love interest and isn't given much depth besides being really into the Zodiac.

characters.name: Wesley "Wes"
characters.description: A car mechanic who Carly initially finds annoying because he tells her that her beloved car can't be salvaged. After facing her fear of replacing her car, Carly warms up to him and they decide to start dating. Betty and Veronica: The cautious and practical "Betty" to Beau's fun-loving and spontaneous "Veronica" and Carly's "Archie". However, Carly finds Beau easier to get along with than Wes, because of how similar in personality she and Beau are. Took a Level in Jerkass: Due to being dumped off-screen by Carly in between Seasons 1 and 2, he and Beau aremuchmore petty and rude towards her in "iGuessEveryone Just Hates Me Now", to the point of the two making a webshow dedicated to badmouthing Carly, and mocking her for being unable to find love when she tries to make amends with them. Unlike Beau however, Wes still displayssomekindness to Carly, praising her on stream for defending Freddie's new girlfriend, Pearl, as well as refusing to go along with Beau's misogynistic rant about how all women are the same.

characters.name: Gwendolyn "Gwen" Mitchell
characters.description: Absurdly Youthful Mother: Millicent is 11 years old at the start of the series, and while Gwen's age is never given, she's presumably around Freddie's age (mid-late twenties), which would mean she had Millicent when she was ateenager. Amicable Exes: She and Freddie are on good terms, with Freddie even adopting her daughter Millicent. Brainy Brunette: Has black hair and is a marine biologist.

characters.name: Tinsley
characters.description: Drama Queen: Has a tendency to act over dramatic, especially when someone isn't accepting a gift from her. Loving Bully: According to Harper, Tinsley used to bully her when they were in school, though Tinsley claims that Harper gave as good as she got. Either way, they end up kissing and dating each other. Upper-Class Twit: She's a rich girl who's quite naive when it comes to interacting with the "common folk".

characters.name: Melanie Puckett
characters.description: Sam's bubbly twin sister. Boarding School: According to Sam in Melanie's debut episode, Melanie's aScholarship Studentat some fancy boarding school. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: The Responsible to Sam's Foolish. Melanie is a hard-working girl who got a scholarship to a private school, treats people well and stays out of trouble. Sam is a bad student and a borderline-sociopathic bully who has frequent run-ins with the law. Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: She's a blonde like her sister, but she's a lot nicer than Sam. Nice Girl: Unlike Sam, she isn't aggressive in the slightest and is kind to everyone.

characters.name: Pam Puckett
characters.description: Sam's terrible mother. Abusive Parents: Seems to be verbally and/or emotionally abusive to Sam. She often gets into arguments with her and once asked her why she couldn't be more like her sister, Melanie. She even fed a baby Sam bottles of black coffee, something very unhealthy for babies, just because it made Sam dance funny. Alliterative Name:PamPucket. Cerebus Retcon: The previous 'wacky' behaviour Sam talks about is retconned into something serious andPlayed for Dramawhen she shows up in her episodeiSam's Mom. Fan Disservice: Implied in "iFight Shelby Marx".Sam:Aw man! Did someone post more pics of my mom in her bikini?

characters.name: Shelby Marx
characters.description: A fifteen-year-old undefeated MMA fighter who challenges Carly to an exhibition fight for charity. Action Girl: She's an MMA fighter. Ambiguously Gay: While nothing about Shelby's sexuality is hinted at, she does have a few subtext moments with Carly and fits a fewButch Lesbianstereotypes. Identical Stranger: Victoria portrayed her before going on to star inher own showthat has since crossed over with this one. Lampshaded by Sam and Freddie, who also stated that Toriwas hotter than Shelby. Nice Girl: Underneath the boxing exterior, Shelby is actually a decent, reasonable gal while promising Carly their fight is more for show and she won't get hurt. That aside,just don't trash talk her or seemingly hurt her family. She even manages to avertCassandra Truthtwice in Carly's case. First, Carly explains the reason she trash talked was becauseSamthought it what she should do, and then was pushed onto her grandmother on accident. Shelby takes her word. Later on, Carly reveals that Nevel made a fake video to trick Shelby into believing Carly tackled her grandmother on purpose so he could "watch [Carly] bleed and cry" following himJumping Off the Slippery Slopein his ongoing war with Carly.

characters.name: Wade Collins
characters.description: A mean singer who guilts Carly and Sam into helping him produce a music video and verbally abuses them throughout the process. Accidental Misnaming: Pronounced "Gibby" as "Jib-lee." Gibby's okay with it. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: At first, he seems like a meek and mild-manneredNice Guywho's trying his best to look out for his sick mother. Needless to say, that impression of him ends up completely shattered by the episode's ending. Catchphrase Insult: He constantly calls people "hobknockers". Child Hater: He says he does in one of his rants during the end.As well as teenagers,animals, and America.

characters.name: Reuben
characters.description: Gibby's bizarre friend who speaks a language only Gibby understands. Abhorrent Admirer: He has a massive, obvious crush on Sam, and she finds him repulsive—when Sam finds out Carly scheduled a triple-date involving her and Reuben, she's appalled. All Love Is Unrequited: He's hung up on Sam for the majority of "iWin a Date," but she rejects him at the Cheesecake Warehouse and sends him running away in tears. Cloud Cuckoolander: Reuben speaks exclusively in nonsensical metaphors that have no apparent meaning, but speak to a rather unique philosophy on life. Ice-Cream Koan: Many of his strange ramblings almost sound profound. For example, "It's like you were born sideways over the moon with two and a half scoops. Sha-pang."

characters.name: Missy Robinson
characters.description: Carly's former best friend who returns to reclaim her former position from Sam. Bitch Alert: After she clearly states what her intentions are, she gives Sam an evil smirk when the two are at school. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: She's all nice and sweet when Carly is around. Childhood Friends: Missy and Carly were friends when their parents were placed at the same Naval base when they were children. Dislikes the New Guy: She hates Sam, and makes it very clear what her intentions are, when she comes back.

characters.name: Lauren Ackerman
characters.description: The former U.S. history teacher for the iCarly trio who has emotional problems. Ax-Crazy: Even if she didn’t kill anyone that we know of (despite having tried to strangle Spencer), she’s still a deeply disturbed woman who is driven to hurt others, in this case her students. Beyond Redemption: She is a total lost cause who isn't even worth helping to find love again when Spencer becomes miserable around her. Her getting arrested is a huge relief for the gang. Cool Teacher: Only when she’s in a good mood. She gives her students A pluses and caramel apples, and she even invites them to go ice skating with her and Spencer. Double Standard: Abuse, Female on Male: Played with. While much of Lauren's instability in regards to her relationship with Spencer isPlayed for Laughs, their relationship is still accurately portrayed as toxic and unhealthy.

characters.name: Chip Chambers
characters.description: The younger brother of Chuck Chambers. His goal is to get revenge on Spencer for getting Chuck sent to military school. Big Brother Instinct: Inverted. Chip is Chuck's younger brother who decides to take revenge on Spencer by screwing with him and screwing with Carly as a form of poetic justice: since Chuck messed with Spencer, as Chuck's younger sibling Chip will screw with Spencer's younger sibling Carly. Cousin Oliver: His introduction was likely due to Chuck's actor having outgrown his role. Enfant Terrible: He's even worse than Chuck. Chuck at least left Carly out of his feud with Spencer. Chip on the other hand is fine with targetinganyoneand even uses worse methods than what Chuck uses to torment others. Faux Affably Evil: He can be polite, but it is completely superficial. This is shown when he gets stuck on the elevator and Spencer encounters him. He tries to act friendly to get Spencer to get him off, but Spencer was not having it and left him there.

characters.name: Steven Shay
characters.description: Carly and Spencer's father and a United States Air Force Colonel. For most of the series, he is on tour on a submarine. Locked Out of the Loop: Unlike his father, Colonel Shay has no idea that Spencer has dropped out of law school before the series begins. When he finally finds out, he nearly strangles his son. Parental Favoritism: While not the abusive kind, it is clear that he loves Carly more than Spencer. Truth in Television: Many question as to why a USAF colonel is onboard a Navy submarine. Dan Schneider clears the confusion by explaining that military officers have more leeway in working with other branches. Unseen No More: Because of his work, Colonel Shay spends most of his time away from his children but are still able to keep contact with them. He finally makes an appearance in the series finale.

characters.name: Mrs. Shay
characters.description: Carly and Spencer's mother and wife of Colonel Shay. While she was never mentioned in the original series, the third season of the revival reveals that she walked out on the family prior to the events of the original series, only to reunite with her children at the end of the episode Ambiguously Absent Parent: Mrs. Shay was never even mentioned in the original series. This ends up being averted in the revival's Season 3 finale, where it is revealed that she walked out on the family before the events of the original series, only to show up at the end of the episode. Harper even lampshades this revelation by saying, "You got a mom?" Missing Mom: Before the Season 3 finale of the revival, Mrs. Shay didn't only just not appear in the show, but was never even talked about. In the revival, she is revealed to have walked out on her family when Carly was still very young. Parental Abandonment: Prior to the original series, Mrs. Shay walked out on her family for reasons yet to be revealed. Because of this, Spencer had tobecome Carly's legal guardiansince their dad was busy with his military obligations.
